data.title: Key facts: Exelon Upholds EPS; PECO Drops Filings; ComEd 19GW Target
data.description:
• Exelon (EXC) reaffirmed 2026 adjusted operating EPS guidance of $2.81–$2.91 and expects cumulative annualized adjusted operating earnings growth 2025–2029 near the top of its 5%–7% range.1
  • Exelon’s PECO asked the Pennsylvania utility commission to withdraw its March 30, 2026 electric and gas rate cases. PECO will reassess capital plans, future filings, and service priorities.2
  • Exelon (EXC) says ComEd targets 19 GW incremental Illinois electric load by 2030; disclosure came via CEO comment, not a regulatory filing.3

data.title: Key facts: Citizens Financial Q1 Net Income $517M; Non-core Down to $2.0B
data.description:
• Citizens Financial reported Q1 2026 net income $517M (+39% YoY), EPS $1.13 (+47%). NIM expanded, interest income and capital markets fees rose, credit quality improved, buybacks and dividend paid.123
  • Citizens Financial cut its non-core portfolio from ~$2.5B in Q4 2025 to ~$2.0B in Q1 2026, targeting about $1.1B by year-end 2026.4

data.title: Key facts: Citigroup Q1 Cards Improve; Hired for Uganda €2.7B Project
data.description:
• Citigroup Q1 results show resilient U.S. consumer spending and improved card credit metrics—lower delinquencies and net charge-offs—indicating stabilizing borrower credit for traders to watch.1
  • Uganda hired Citigroup to raise financing for its €2.7B Standard Gauge Railway project; Citigroup is in talks with the World Bank about potential funding support.2

data.title: Key facts: Boeing hires 100–140 weekly; scales space w/ Millennium; El Al orders 6 787‑9s
data.description:
• Boeing hiring 100–140 factory workers weekly in Puget Sound—the fastest since 2024—to replace retirees and sustain higher output for 737 MAX North Line, 777X assembly, parts and tooling.1
  • Boeing (BA) and Millennium plan to expand space-production capacity, scale satellite output, broaden product lineup and improve manufacturing consistency to meet rising defense and commercial demand.2
  • Boeing: El Al ordered six more 787-9s, added options for up to six additional 787s and upgraded some orders to 787-10s — updates affect BA fleet demand and order backlog.3

data.title: Key facts: Baird trims MSFT target; AI doubts; credit pause; 22% CAGR
data.description:
• Baird cut Microsoft price target to $500 from $540, kept Buy rating, citing mixed sentiment on Copilot and rising AI competition; expects Copilot traction and solid Azure trends to support earnings.12
  • Investor sentiment shifted after Anthropic’s Mythos disclosure raised questions about Microsoft’s AI positioning and possible AI-driven changes to software workflows, affecting market views on MSFT.3
  • Microsoft paused new carbon removal credit purchases this week, telling some suppliers the pause may change purchase timing or volume but does not alter its overall carbon removal goals.4
  • Since Satya Nadella became CEO in Feb 2014, Microsoft (MSFT) stock has returned a 22% compound annual growth rate, highlighting multi-year share performance under his tenure.5

data.title: Key facts: Ally Q1 Revenue $2.17B Beats Estimates; $0.30 Dividend
data.description:
• Ally Financial reported Q1 revenue $2.17B, +3.7% YoY, and beat EPS and revenue estimates. Stock rose ~13.8% past month; peers' sector gained ~9.5%.1
  • Ally Financial (ALLY) set a $0.30 quarterly cash dividend per common share payable May 15, 2026 to holders of record May 1, 2026; also declared preferred dividends payable May 15, 2026.2

data.title: Key facts: Abbott Q1 diagnostics -7.4%; guidance cut; Exact Sciences
data.description:
• Abbott (ABT) Q1 sales fell; rapid and molecular diagnostics down 7.4% to $812M, weaker nutrition and diabetes device sales, heart devices lost share to Edwards. Shares fell ~4–6% after outlook cut.1
  • Abbott Laboratories (ABT) Q1 CY2026 adjusted EPS $1.15, in line with estimates. Full-year adjusted EPS guidance cut to $5.48 midpoint, a 3.4% reduction from prior outlook.2
  • Abbott (ABT) says Exact Sciences deal boosted its oncology diagnostics and helped growth; Medical Devices and Established Pharmaceuticals segments showed solid performance.3

data.title: Key facts: AMD to Supply 6 GW GPUs to Meta; Stock Shows Bullish
data.description:
• AMD will supply up to 6 GW of GPUs, including MI450-based custom AI accelerators, to Meta for AI workloads, with a large-scale rollout planned in H2 2026 and performance-linked incentives.1
  • AMD stock cleared key moving averages; RSI near 70s, bullish MACD. Options flow favors calls and momentum indicators show strong uptrend—relevant technical cues for traders.2

data.title: Smart Powerr Secures $1.05M Note, Lines Up Additional $9.05M With Streeterville Capital
data.description:
Smart Powerr entered into financing with Streeterville Capital, closing a $1.05 million secured A-1 note at 8% interest and 24-month maturity, yielding $1.0 million in cash proceeds after discount and fees. The agreement also sets up additional financing: a prospective $1.05 million A-2 note and an $8.0 million secured B note, subject to conditions. The B note proceeds are to be held under a deposit account control agreement at Lakeside Bank, backed by a subsidiary guaranty and equity pledge. Covenants limit certain new debt and liens until repayment, supporting liquidity and project funding.

Agreement 1: Smart Powerr Raises $1.05 Million via 8% Secured Note From Streeterville Capital

  • Agreement type: Secured promissory note (A-1), 8% interest, 24-month maturity
  • Counterparty: Streeterville Capital
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 10 2026 / Apr 10 2026
  • Duration / Termination: 24 months
  • Reason: Bolster liquidity for operations and growth

Agreement 2: Smart Powerr Lines Up Additional $9.05 Million Notes, Including $8 Million Secured B Note

  • Agreement type: Note purchase agreement for A-2 and secured B notes
  • Counterparty: Streeterville Capital
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 10 2026 / Apr 10 2026
  • Duration / Termination: Until obligations repaid; covenants in effect
  • Reason: Provide additional funding and secure project capital

Original SEC Filing: Smart Powerr Corp. [ CREG ] - 8-K - Apr. 16, 2026

data.title: Bank posts Q1 2026 net income $20.0M, adjusted EPS $2.24; declares $0.55 dividend
data.description:
Bank reported net income of $20.0 million, or $1.78 per share, for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, and adjusted net income (non-GAAP) of $25.1 million, or adjusted diluted earnings of $2.24 per share after removing acquisition-related expenses and asset sale impacts. Total assets rose to $6.07 billion following the January 1 acquisition of Centre, which added roughly $1.48 billion of assets. The board declared a quarterly cash dividend of $0.55 per share, payable July 8, 2026.

Financial Highlights

  • Net income (GAAP): $19.99 million for Q1 2026; basic and diluted EPS: $1.78.
  • Adjusted net income (non-GAAP): $25.08 million for Q1 2026; adjusted earnings per share (non-GAAP): $2.24.
  • Net interest income: $53.22 million for Q1 2026; net interest margin (taxable equivalent): 3.96%.
  • Noninterest income: $10.53 million; noninterest expense: $39.06 million for Q1 2026.
  • Total assets: $6.07 billion at March 31, 2026; stockholders’ equity: $819.85 million; tangible book value per share (non-GAAP): $47.04.

Business Highlights

  • Completed acquisition of Centre on January 1, 2026, adding First National Bank and Trust operations and creating a new Stateline region.
  • Acquisition added approximately $1.48 billion in assets, ~$981.5 million in loans (reduced to $936.7 million by quarter end) and ~$1.38 billion in deposits.
  • Integration actions included permanent closure of six overlapping branches and plans to build new offices in Walworth, Delavan, and Monroe; core data processing conversion scheduled for completion in Q2 2026.
  • Expanded service capabilities with added Trust and Wealth Management, Fraud, and Treasury Management teams from the acquisition; Trust and Wealth Management generated $1.58 million in noninterest income in Q1 2026.
  • Operational adjustments in the acquired portfolio led to a slight loan portfolio contraction in the Stateline region as Bank transitioned out of balances not aligned with its lending philosophy.

Original SEC Filing: Bank First Corp [ BFC ] - 8-K - Apr. 16, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: U.S. Stocks Extend Rally — Market Talk
data.description:
1604 ET - U.S. stocks post modest gains with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq Composite setting fresh record highs for the second straight session. A temporary ceasefire deal between Israel and Lebanon lift sentiment that a broader agreement to end the war with Iran is possible. Energy and real estate are the top gaining sectors while health care stocks perform the worst. Abbott Laboratories loses 6% after cutting its full-year earnings outlook. PepsiCo shares rise 2.3% as price cuts led to higher sales. DJIA gains 115 points, or 0.2%, to 48578, the S&P 500 rises 0.3% to 7041 and the Nasdaq climbs 0.4% to 24102. (patrick.sullivan@wsj.com)

1548 ET - Treasury yields rise amid apparent progress in peace negotiations and uplifting U.S. economic indicators. President Trump says a cease-fire between Israel and Lebanon starts today, and that a peace agreement with Iran is "very close." Jobless claims decrease, indicating layoffs remain at bay. The Philadelphia Fed's business activity index outperforms expectations. March industrial production, however, declines. Futures price a lengthy Fed hold. The 10-year rises 0.029 percentage point to 4.308% and the two-year adds 0.014 p.p. to 3.777%. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1540 ET - Most of the Fed's easing is "in the rearview mirror" and "we are still waiting for that impact to emerge," Truist's Chip Hughey says. He expects the Fed to stay on hold for now, while one or two cuts later this year are possible. That would add up to half of a percentage point, compared to 1.75 p.p. cut between September and December. "We'll get some relief but probably not a tremendous amount of relief," he says. Hughey expects Treasury yields to remain elevated as markets fear inflation stemming from the war in Iran will keep the Fed from cutting anytime soon. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1534 ET - The 10-year Treasury yield is unlikely to surge past 4.5%, Truist's Chip Hughey says.Yields spiked last month as the war in Iran boosted oil prices. The 10-year peaked at 4.44% on March 27 and remains above pre-war levels. "Locking in longer-dated fixed income became more attractive with that really abrupt spike in yields," Hughey says. He adds that the type of inflation concerns that would push the benchmark yield above 4.5% would also trigger growth concerns that typically have the opposite effect. That high level could also push investors to sell stocks and buy bonds, also capping yields. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1530 ET - Flutter's growth prospects in the U.S. are looking less promising, Citibank analysts say. The analysts downgrade Flutter's stock to sell from buy as they are losing conviction about the company's 2026 and 2027 growth forecasts for its U.S. business. Using state data, the analysts estimate Flutter's handle growth will decline sequentially. Its prediction markets product is also not creating a huge opportunity, as the business is still nascent and there is regulatory uncertainty about prediction markets as a whole, the analysts say. (katherine.hamilton@wsj.com)

1527 ET - Artificial-intelligence is enabling developers to produce software faster than their companies can review and ship it, Morgan Stanley analysts write in a note, creating strain on debugging and code review for all but a handful of high-performing firms. This bottleneck highlights the importance of agentic AI in the development process, both in standard review and release processes and in addressing the heightened security exposure from AI-generated code, the analysts write. "Agentic AI is emerging as the next logical layer of automation," they write. "The competitive battleground is therefore shifting from tools that help developers write code to platforms that can govern and automate the broader software lifecycle, with adoption likely to depend on strong oversight, policy controls, and auditability." (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

1525 ET - A cease-fire deal between Israel and Lebanon could support demand for Treasurys and weigh down on yields, to the extent that it points to broader peace in the Middle East, Truist's Chip Hughey says. "That is a positive step towards a more durable resolution" to the conflict between U.S., Iran and Israel, he says. Peace will likely entail a lengthy process, though. "The events in Iran are not going to wrap up in the very, very near term." Concerns about the conflict's duration and its inflationary impact are applying upward pressure in yields today, he says. (paulo.trevisani@wsj.com; @ptrevisani)

1515 ET - Morgan Stanley's prediction that artificial-intelligence will support growth in developer seats rather than lead to a contraction seems to be playing out, analysts write in a note. Job posting trends appear to show resilient demand for developers, particularly mid-career and senior positions, compared to the broader labor market, they write. "As development becomes cheaper and faster, organizations are building more software, which helps offset pressure on headcount by expanding the number of projects that are economically viable," the analysts write. "Even against a backdrop of prominent layoffs, the underlying signals point to a broader and more leveraged developer base." (elias.schisgall@wsj.com)

1441 ET - Ulta Beauty is doubling down on earlier-stage and indie brands as part of its focus on providing more new products, Raymond James analysts say in a note after meeting with management. The shift should boost Ulta's lineup of differentiated and exclusive products, and better position it as a launch partner for brands, rather than as a channel for them to scale, the analysts say. The focus on newer brands may come at a detriment to larger brands if they fall behind on offering new or exclusive products, the analysts say. The shift is one of several underway at Ulta that the analysts see improving the company's growth perspectives and competitive positioning, they say. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1342 ET - PPG's move to raise prices by up to 20% could indicate further pain ahead for consumers due to the war in Iran, as increased prices for raw materials, energy, logistics and packaging spread and eventually filter down to shoppers, Mizuho says in a note. The analysts point to potential headwinds for retailers like Home Depot and Lowe's in particular, which sell products similar to PPG's paints, coatings and other specialty products. "While price increases can typically aid sales growth, demand needs to be in the right spot," the analysts say. "Layering on more inflation at a time of subdued home-related activity and elevated rates could act as another weight on the sector's recovery." PepsiCo also recently warned of further inflation to come, they say. (kelly.cloonan@wsj.com)

1336 ET - Cryptocurrency and the blockchain is expected to take on a central role in the expansion of money apps by social media giants like X and Meta, says Grayscale in a note. The firm points to the introduction of "smart cashtags" on X as the next major step in the integration of crypto infrastructure with the other roles social media plays. Grayscale points to China's WeChat as an implementation of the "everything" app that X looks to follow, and may signal the direction companies like Meta and PayPal take with their offerings. Bitcoin is down 0.9% today to $74,133, according to data from LSEG. (kirk.maltais@wsj.com)

1317 ET - U.S. crude futures are adding to gains after U.S. officials said the blockade of ships in and out of Iran applies to vessels of all nationalities and gave no signs the operation will end anytime soon. Prospects for further U.S.-Iran talks have reduced volatility in recent sessions. "What the market is ultimately pricing right now is a variety of potentially quite different outcomes," says Ben Hoff of Societe Generale. "Some of them are probably somewhat more optimistic and others more pessimistic." Pricing in futures "is a reflection of some kind of market-weighted probability of those different outcomes," he adds. WTI is up 3.6% at $94.60 a barrel and Brent is 4.8% higher at $99.45. (anthony.harrup@wsj.com)

data.title: Allbirds Surges 582% Then Drops 31% After AI Rebrand Pivot
data.description:
Allbirds is starting to look like a case study in how quickly AI-driven enthusiasm can both inflate and unwind. The company's plan to pivot from wool footwear into artificial intelligence infrastructure under a NewBird AI rebrand initially triggered a surge of more than 582%, accompanied by an extraordinary spike in trading volume to nearly 300 million shares versus a typical daily average just above 20 million. But the follow-through didn't hold. By Thursday, shares had fallen as much as 31%, suggesting the early reaction could be more about positioning and momentum than conviction in a long-term shift.

From a fundamental standpoint, the response from market observers leans cautious. Adam Sarhan of 50 Park Investments framed the move as resembling a meme-stock setup, where price action is driven more by sentiment than by a clearly defined AI capability. That view was reinforced by William Blair analyst Dylan Carden, who described the rally as by any measure a Hail Mary, attributing it to a combination of shallow float, automated momentum, and elevated hype. The backdrop is difficult to ignore: the stock has declined about 96% since its 2021 IPO, which could suggest the pivot is being interpreted as an attempt to reset expectations rather than evidence of an established competitive position in AI.

The broader setup may be just as important as the company-specific story. Similar bursts of interest have appeared before, from pandemic-era meme names like GameStop and AMC Entertainment to more recent crypto-linked spikes in smaller companies. Even this week, Myseum, a social-media company with a market capitalization just over $6 million, saw its shares move higher after announcing an AI-focused rebrand. For investors, the pattern could point to pockets of speculative behavior building again, particularly around AI, where narrative-driven moves can develop quickly and, in some cases, reverse just as fast.

data.title: Novo Workforce Changes May Signal Ongoing Reset
data.description:
Novo Nordisk is bringing people back in, adding around 2,000 employees this year after going through a pretty significant reset.

On the surface, it looks like a hiring push, but it is not that simple. According to Bloomberg, about 1,400 of those hires have already joined, including nearly 400 in Denmark. At the same time, people are still leaving. Novo has a 5% voluntary attrition rate, and overall turnover was closer to 18% in 2025, so a lot of this hiring is really about filling gaps rather than growing the team.

That makes more sense when you look at last year. The company ended 2025 with around 69,505 employees, after cutting roughly 7,800 roles, or about 10% of its workforce, during a major restructuring under new CEO Mike Doustdar. Those cuts were not just at the edges either, they reached senior levels, which shows how deep the changes were.

data.title: Third Century Bancorp Q1 net income rises on strong credit quality, expense control
data.description:
Overview

  • US community bank's Q1 net income rose 35% yr/yr, with EPS at $0.52

  • Total interest income increased $387,000 yr/yr, driven by higher average loan and cash balances

  • Company cites strong credit quality and expense control as supporting quarterly results

Outlook

  • Company did not provide specific guidance or outlook for future periods

Result Drivers

  • TOTAL INTEREST INCOME - Higher average loan and cash balances led to increased total interest income, per CEO David A. Coffey

  • CREDIT QUALITY - Strong credit quality with no non-performing loans supported results, according to CEO David A. Coffey

  • EXPENSE CONTROL - Continued expense control and reduced use of higher-cost wholesale funding contributed to quarterly performance, per CEO David A. Coffey
